automotive-ev-battery.title: EIB loans billion-euro sum for Hamburg's distribution grid and electric buses
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The European Investment Bank is providing up to one billion euros to modernize Hamburg's electricity distribution grid, with an additional 125 million euros allocated for electric bus procurement by the city's transport operator. This significant investment underscores major commitment to electrifying urban public transportation and upgrading critical grid infrastructure in Europe's major ports.

automotive-ev-battery.title: ChargePoint and OBE Power plan 2,500 chargers for multifamily housing
automotive-ev-battery.summary: ChargePoint has partnered with OBE Power to deploy approximately 2,500 electric vehicle charging ports across multifamily residential properties in North America, with installations beginning in 2026. This partnership addresses a critical gap in EV charging accessibility for apartment and multi-unit dwelling residents.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kempower supplies fast chargers for APM Terminals globally
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Finnish charging infrastructure manufacturer Kempower has signed a global framework agreement with port operator APM Terminals to supply fast-charging solutions across port facilities worldwide. Pilot projects are already operational in Mexico, Morocco, and Peru, marking expansion of EV charging infrastructure into the maritime logistics sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Toyota reveals UK pricing for electric Hilux
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Toyota has announced UK pricing and specifications for its first battery-electric Hilux, positioned as the brand's entry into the electric pickup truck segment at £57,845. Order books open June 1 with customer deliveries scheduled to commence later that month.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Zunder launches charging infrastructure software Kawat for third parties
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Spanish charging network operator Zunder is commercializing its proprietary Kawat software platform to enable third-party companies to manage large-scale charging infrastructure networks. The move democratizes access to enterprise-grade charging management technology developed from Zunder's operation of one of Southern Europe's largest ultra-fast charging networks.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Stellantis to support Voyah expansion in Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Stellantis and Dongfeng have announced an intensive European partnership centered on Dongfeng's Voyah brand, with plans to manufacture Dongfeng vehicles at Stellantis' Rennes plant. This collaboration represents a strategic move to bring Chinese electric vehicle brands into the competitive European market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Audi tests A2 E-Tron ahead of autumn premiere
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Audi is conducting extensive testing of its new A2 e-tron entry-level electric vehicle ahead of its official autumn 2026 debut, with trials underway in Nordic regions, wind tunnels, and public roads. The compact electric model represents Audi's strategy to compete in the affordable EV segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Peugeot opens UK order books for revised E-408
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Peugeot has opened UK order books for its revised 408 range, with the fully electric E-408 fastback priced from £34,195. The competitively positioned electric vehicle expands Peugeot's EV offerings in the UK market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Study highlights potential of solar modules on vehicle roofs
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The SolarMoves pilot project concluded that vehicle-integrated solar modules can provide significant energy generation, covering up to 55% of energy demand in Central Europe and up to 80% in Southern Europe. The research suggests particularly strong commercial applications for the logistics sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Autel Energy Europe highlights integrated charging solutions for growing infrastructure demands
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Autel Energy Europe presented its latest MaxiCharger AC and DC charging portfolio at the Amsterdam summit, emphasizing integrated solutions designed to address increasing European infrastructure demands for reliability and operational efficiency. The company showcased both centralized and distributed charging architectures alongside supporting software and service capabilities.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran says no surrender to US, diplomacy 'wiser' than war
world-news.summary: Iran has rejected capitulation to the United States and signaled openness to diplomatic resolution amid ongoing regional military tensions. Israeli operations have resulted in over 3,000 deaths across Lebanon since March, according to Lebanese health authorities, escalating Middle Eastern instability.

world-news.title: Rosenberg: Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
world-news.summary: Russia and China demonstrated unified positioning on the global stage during diplomatic engagement, though negotiations revealed limitations in their strategic alignment. The absence of a pipeline agreement underscores underlying tensions despite public displays of solidarity.

world-news.title: US charges Cuba's Raúl Castro with murder over 1996 downing of two planes
world-news.summary: The United States has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro and five others for conspiracy to murder US nationals, murder, and destruction of aircraft related to the 1996 shootdown of civilian planes. The charges revive a decades-old Cold War-era incident, marking a significant escalation in US-Cuba judicial tensions.

world-news.title: Austrian ex-intelligence officer found guilty of Russia spying charges
world-news.summary: An Austrian former intelligence official has been convicted of espionage charges related to Russian intelligence activities. The verdict reinforces concerns about Austria's vulnerability to Russian intelligence operations and broader European security threats.

world-news.title: Ebola vaccine could take nine months as death toll rises further, WHO warns
world-news.summary: The World Health Organization reports that an Ebola vaccine deployment may require nine months while the outbreak continues to expand. Current figures indicate 600 confirmed cases and 139 suspected deaths, prompting urgent global health response measures.

world-news.title: Bolivian president to reshuffle cabinet amid antigovernment protests
world-news.summary: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz is undertaking a cabinet reorganization in response to sustained civil unrest and deteriorating economic conditions. The political instability reflects broader regional challenges facing South American governance.

world-news.title: US condemns Israel's Ben-Gvir while sanctioning Gaza flotilla organisers
world-news.summary: The United States issued diplomatic criticism of Israeli minister Itamar Ben-Gvir's treatment of Gaza flotilla activists while simultaneously imposing sanctions on the flotilla organizers. The contradictory positions reflect tensions in US Middle East policy and responses to Israeli-Palestinian conflict dynamics.

world-news.title: US lifts sanctions on Francesca Albanese, UN expert on Palestinian rights
world-news.summary: The United States has reversed sanctions against Francesca Albanese, a United Nations expert on Palestinian rights who had been sanctioned for advocacy opposing Israel's military operations in Gaza. The reversal signals a policy shift regarding international human rights advocacy.

world-news.title: US military jets and drones tracked near Cuba as tensions rise
world-news.summary: US Navy reconnaissance aircraft and unmanned systems have conducted multiple operations near Cuban territory over the past week, according to tracking data. The surveillance activity coincides with elevated bilateral tensions following the indictment of former Cuban leader Raúl Castro.

world-news.title: Far-right Israeli minister condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
world-news.summary: International governments including France and Italy have criticized Israeli minister Itamar Ben-Gvir for a video showing him taunting detained Gaza flotilla activists at an Israeli port. The incident has drawn widespread diplomatic condemnation over treatment of humanitarian activists.

tech-ai.title: The SpaceX IPO filing is filled with AI bets, Starship dreams, and Elon Musk at the center
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX has filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC for what could be the largest initial public offering ever, with the company generating $18.67 billion in revenue in 2025 primarily from its Starlink satellite service. The filing reveals significant AI investments and establishes Elon Musk as CEO, CTO, and chairman, marking a major milestone in the aerospace company's path to public markets.

tech-ai.title: xAI burned $6.4B last year — SpaceX's IPO filing shows why the spending is far from over
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals that Elon Musk's xAI lost $6.4 billion in 2025 while planning a massive expansion of its Grok AI service, offering the first public financial disclosure of Musk's AI ambitions. The filing indicates continued substantial investment in xAI infrastructure and development despite the significant losses.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25B per month for compute
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has agreed to pay xAI $1.25 billion monthly for computing resources, representing a significant strategic partnership in the AI industry. The deal underscores the growing capital intensity of developing large language models and the importance of securing reliable compute infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic says it's about to have its first profitable quarter
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has informed its investors that it will more than double revenue to approximately $10.9 billion in its second quarter, signaling a major milestone toward profitability for the AI startup. This projection represents significant commercial traction for the company's Claude AI model and services.

tech-ai.title: Jensen Huang says he's found a 'brand new' $200B market for Nvidia
tech-ai.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has identified a new $200 billion market opportunity centered on CPUs for AI agents, positioning the company to expand beyond its traditional GPU-focused business. The projection highlights Nvidia's strategic focus on capturing value throughout the AI infrastructure and application stack.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia posts another record quarter, reveals $43B of holdings in startups
tech-ai.summary: Nvidia announced record revenue results but cautioned that growth would decelerate in the following quarter, while also disclosing $43 billion in holdings across startup investments. The company's portfolio reveals its diverse bets across emerging AI and technology companies.

tech-ai.title: Musk's xAI is being sued over its data center generators — now it's buying $2.8B more
tech-ai.summary: xAI has announced plans to purchase $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over the next three years for its data centers, according to SpaceX's IPO filing, despite facing ongoing litigation over previous generator installations. The massive infrastructure investment underscores the energy demands of scaling AI operations.

tech-ai.title: You can now remix other people's YouTube Shorts with AI
tech-ai.summary: Google has launched a new YouTube Shorts Remix feature powered by Gemini Omni that allows users to restyling clips and insert themselves into other people's videos using AI-generated effects. The feature enables users to transform videos into various artistic styles such as pixel art, anime, or horror film formats, reflecting broader AI integration into content creation platforms.

tech-ai.title: Sam Altman makes 'mic drop' offer to every Y Combinator startup
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that OpenAI will invest in every startup in the current Y Combinator class through a tokens-for-equity arrangement, signaling aggressive market expansion. The offer represents a significant initiative to embed OpenAI's technology deeply within the startup ecosystem.

tech-ai.title: Samsung has a tentative deal with workers to avoid a memory chip strike
tech-ai.summary: Samsung Electronics has reached a tentative agreement with its union to prevent an planned 18-day strike by over 47,000 workers at domestic chipmaking plants. The deal comes amid concerns about semiconductor supply chain disruptions and highlights labor tensions within the critical memory chip manufacturing sector.

science-space.title: NASA to Provide Update on Moon Base Strategy, Missions
science-space.summary: NASA will host a news conference on May 26 to share comprehensive Moon Base plans and highlight progress toward establishing a sustained human presence on the lunar surface. The briefing will include discussions of program progress, new industry partnerships, and upcoming mission details from agency leadership.

science-space.title: NASA Releases Technology Priorities to Energize Space Industry
science-space.summary: NASA released the 2026 Civil Space Shortfall Ranking list, integrating over 400 responses from industry, government, and academia to identify critical technology gaps. The document prioritizes technology areas requiring further development to support future exploration, science missions, and other strategic objectives.

science-space.title: Scientists find a hidden route to the moon that saves fuel
science-space.summary: An international research team has identified a more efficient trajectory to the moon using advanced computer modeling, potentially reducing fuel consumption for lunar missions. The discovery could significantly impact the cost and feasibility of future lunar exploration efforts.

science-space.title: Psyche Spacecraft Completes Mars Flyby
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft successfully completed a close approach to Mars on May 15, passing within 2,864 miles of the planet's surface and capturing detailed images including the double-ring Huygens crater. The flyby served as a gravity assist maneuver for the spacecraft's ongoing mission to the metallic asteroid Psyche.

science-space.title: NASA-developed AI Could Help Track Harmful Algae
science-space.summary: NASA scientists have developed an artificial intelligence tool capable of detecting harmful algal blooms by fusing data from multiple satellites, as demonstrated in a study published in AGU Earth and Space Science. The technology successfully identified blooms in western Florida and Southern California, offering potential solutions to a persistent environmental challenge.

science-space.title: NASA TechLeap Prize: Robotically Manipulated Payload Challenge
science-space.summary: NASA has launched the fifth in its TechLeap Prize series, challenging applicants to develop payloads that can be manipulated by robotic arms in low Earth orbit. The competition aims to advance persistent infrastructure for in-space servicing, assembly, and manufacturing, with up to three winners receiving substantial funding.

science-space.title: NASA's Fermi Glimpses Power Source of Supercharged Supernovae
science-space.summary: An international research team analyzing data from NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope has identified a rare, unusually luminous supernova powered by a highly magnetized neutron star created during the stellar collapse. The discovery provides new insights into the mechanisms driving some of the most energetic explosions in the universe.

science-space.title: Space debris is forcing satellites to dodge more often — costing us vital science
science-space.summary: Increasing space debris is forcing operational satellites to perform more frequent evasive maneuvers, resulting in lost data collection and precious fuel consumption. Experts warn the situation will deteriorate further before improvement efforts take effect, threatening the sustainability of critical space-based science missions.

science-space.title: NASA Announces Winners in University Aeronautics Competition
science-space.summary: South Dakota State University won first place in NASA's fifth annual Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, which challenged student teams to develop innovative systems and practices for commercial aircraft maintenance. The RepAir competition represents NASA's efforts to advance critical aviation infrastructure through educational partnerships.

science-space.title: Where did Neptune's mysterious moon Nereid come from? It may be the only survivor of the planet's violent history
science-space.summary: New research suggests that Neptune's moon Nereid may be the sole surviving satellite from the planet's original system following violent planetary rearrangement in the early solar system. The study provides new perspectives on the formation and evolution of Neptune's unusual moon system.

finance-markets.title: OpenAI readies IPO filing to list as soon as September
finance-markets.summary: OpenAI is preparing to file for an initial public offering valued at approximately $1 trillion, with Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley serving as lead bankers and Cooley providing legal counsel. The filing represents a major milestone in the AI sector and signals accelerating momentum toward public markets debuts by leading artificial intelligence companies.

finance-markets.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX sets out plans for biggest IPO in history
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX has announced plans for what would be the largest initial public offering in history, positioning itself and other major companies to launch a wave of substantial Wall Street listings. The filing reveals billions in losses while detailing Musk's control structure through super-voting shares.

finance-markets.title: SoftBank Group Shares Surge Almost 20% on OpenAI, SB Energy IPOs
finance-markets.summary: SoftBank Group's shares jumped nearly 20% following news that OpenAI is preparing an IPO filing, reflecting investor enthusiasm for the company's AI investments and subsidiary SB Energy's planned listing. The surge underscores market optimism surrounding the artificial intelligence sector's transition to public markets.

finance-markets.title: Nvidia lifts dividend as investors fret about growth prospects
finance-markets.summary: Nvidia, the world's most valuable company, announced a dividend increase despite reporting better-than-expected revenue and forecasts, as its shares declined amid investor concerns about slowing growth. The mixed market reaction suggests tempered enthusiasm for the AI chipmaker despite solid financial performance.

finance-markets.title: Samsung reaches last-minute deal to avert strike over AI riches
finance-markets.summary: Samsung Electronics reached a tentative agreement with its labor union to prevent a work stoppage that threatened to disrupt both the Korean economy and the global artificial intelligence sector. The settlement averts potential supply chain disruptions in memory chip manufacturing, a critical input for AI infrastructure.

finance-markets.title: AvalonBay, Equity Residential Said to Near Mega-REIT Tie-Up
finance-markets.summary: AvalonBay Communities and Equity Residential, two of the largest apartment real estate investment trusts by market value, are reportedly nearing a merger agreement. The combination would create a significant consolidation in the residential REIT sector.

finance-markets.title: Oil drops nearly 6% as two China-bound supertankers cross Strait of Hormuz
finance-markets.summary: Brent crude fell to approximately $105 per barrel as two supertankers transited the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a potential resumption of energy flows to Asia and easing supply concerns. The decline reflects improved sentiment regarding global oil market stability following recent geopolitical tensions.

finance-markets.title: Anthropic on track for first profitable quarter
finance-markets.summary: Anthropic is poised to achieve profitability in its next quarter, ahead of competitors OpenAI and xAI, demonstrating the AI company's path toward sustainable business operations. The milestone underscores competitive dynamics in the artificial intelligence sector as leading firms navigate growth and profitability tradeoffs.

finance-markets.title: Global Funds Pile Back Into Chinese Stocks in April, Data Shows
finance-markets.summary: Foreign investors returned to Chinese equities in April, signaling renewed interest in the market following earlier selloffs triggered by regional geopolitical events. The inflow suggests stabilizing sentiment toward Chinese assets and broader emerging markets.

finance-markets.title: Private equity's new escape hatch keeps unsold companies in limbo
finance-markets.summary: Growing adoption of so-called CV squared funds by private equity firms reflects a challenging environment for public market exits and fundraising. The trend highlights the current downturn in initial public offerings as alternative strategies emerge to address the backlog of unsold portfolio companies.

crypto-web3.title: Federal Reserve proposes limited master accounts long pursued by crypto firms
crypto-web3.summary: The U.S. Federal Reserve has issued a revised proposal on payment account structure, advancing efforts to establish so-called skinny accounts that crypto firms have advocated for. This represents significant regulatory progress toward providing digital asset companies with direct Federal Reserve access for banking services.

crypto-web3.title: Prediction markets firms take heat in Senate Commerce hearing scrutinizing surge
crypto-web3.summary: Major prediction market platforms including Kalshi faced congressional scrutiny during a Senate Commerce hearing regarding rapid market growth. Lawmakers raised concerns about advertising to minors, potential athlete cheating, and conflicts with regulated gaming frameworks.

crypto-web3.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX held 18,712 bitcoin at fair value of $1.29 billion at end of Q1, IPO filing shows
crypto-web3.summary: SpaceX disclosed holdings of approximately 18,712 bitcoin valued at $1.29 billion in its initial public offering filing, positioning the company as a major institutional cryptocurrency holder. The disclosure underscores growing corporate adoption of bitcoin as SpaceX pursues a public listing at a valuation exceeding $1.5 trillion.

crypto-web3.title: Polymarket moves to list parlays while SEC seeks public input on prediction market ETFs
crypto-web3.summary: Polymarket is expanding its offerings by listing combinatorial outcome contracts, or parlays, that resolve based on multiple underlying contract outcomes. Meanwhile, the SEC is soliciting public feedback on the potential regulation of prediction market-based exchange-traded funds.

crypto-web3.title: Securitize remains in the red even as record quarter fuels public listing plans
crypto-web3.summary: The tokenization platform Securitize reported record quarterly revenue and $3.4 billion in tokenized assets under management but continued posting net losses as it invests in growth ahead of its planned merger with Cantor Equity Partners II. The company is proceeding with public listing preparations despite ongoing profitability challenges.

crypto-web3.title: Latest Congressional swing at crypto tax reform would direct IRS to review de minimis exemptions
crypto-web3.summary: The updated Parity Act includes revised language governing payment stablecoins and mandates that the IRS conduct a comprehensive review of potential de minimis exemptions for cryptocurrency transactions. The proposal represents ongoing legislative efforts to clarify tax treatment for digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto custody firm Copper is looking to sell the company for $500 million
crypto-web3.summary: Copper, a prominent digital asset custody provider, has engaged Cantor Fitzgerald to facilitate a potential sale valued at approximately $500 million. The transaction would represent a significant consolidation in the crypto institutional services sector.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin miners tied to AI rise as Nvidia posts big earnings beat and strong outlook
crypto-web3.summary: Cryptocurrency mining stocks linked to data center and high-performance computing benefited from Nvidia's stronger-than-expected earnings results and optimistic guidance on artificial intelligence demand. The correlation highlights the intersection between AI infrastructure growth and crypto mining economics.

crypto-web3.title: Catena Labs lands $30 million Series A, files for national trust bank charter to underpin agentic finance
crypto-web3.summary: Catena Labs, founded by Sean Neville, secured $30 million in Series A funding and filed for a national trust bank charter to establish regulated infrastructure for artificial intelligence agent financial transactions. The funding positions the firm at the intersection of AI agents and decentralized finance.

crypto-web3.title: Arbitrum-based derivatives venue Variational raises $50 million Series A led by Dragonfly
crypto-web3.summary: Variational, an Arbitrum-based derivatives trading platform, raised $50 million in Series A funding led by venture capital firm Dragonfly. The firm is developing the Omni app aimed at retail traders with zero-fee trading capabilities on decentralized derivatives infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: OpenAI barrels toward IPO that may happen in September
startups-vc.summary: OpenAI is reportedly preparing for an initial public offering that could occur in September, following the dismissal of Elon Musk's lawsuit that had threatened the company's structure and finances. This milestone represents a significant development in the AI sector and broader venture capital landscape.

startups-vc.title: Cerebras Shares Soar In First Day On Nasdaq
startups-vc.summary: AI chip startup Cerebras Systems made its public market debut on the Nasdaq after years of heavy private fundraising and previous IPO plan adjustments. The strong market reception underscores investor appetite for artificial intelligence infrastructure companies.

startups-vc.title: Digital Banking Startup Mercury Lands $200M At $5.2B Valuation Amid Fintech Funding Uptick
startups-vc.summary: Digital banking startup Mercury raised $200 million in Series D funding at a $5.2 billion valuation, representing a 49 percent increase from its previous $3.5 billion valuation. The funding demonstrates robust investor confidence in the fintech sector and digital banking solutions.

startups-vc.title: Sam Altman makes 'mic drop' offer to every Y Combinator startup
startups-vc.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman offered to invest in every startup in the current Y Combinator class through a tokens-for-equity arrangement, signaling OpenAI's aggressive expansion strategy in the startup ecosystem. The offer represents a notable venture capital move by one of the sector's most influential leaders.

startups-vc.title: Embodied AI Fuels Record Robotics Funding In China As IPO Momentum Builds
startups-vc.summary: China-based robotics companies have already raised $5.6 billion across 176 deals in 2026 through mid-May, matching the entire funding total for all of 2021, signaling unprecedented investor enthusiasm for embodied AI and robotics. The surge reflects growing IPO momentum in China's robotics sector.

startups-vc.title: How Lucra raised $20M as an eSports play when every VC only wants AI
startups-vc.summary: An eSports gamification loyalty startup secured $20 million from Cathie Wood's ARK Invest without emphasizing AI, challenging the current market narrative that artificial intelligence is essential for fundraising. The funding demonstrates that non-AI startups can still attract significant institutional capital.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Xpanner Lands $18M To Offer 'Automation As A Service' To Construction Sites
startups-vc.summary: Construction automation startup Xpanner raised $18 million in Series B funding to expand its robotics and physical AI technology that retrofits construction equipment. The investment highlights growing venture capital interest in automating traditionally labor-intensive industries.

startups-vc.title: This startup raised $43M to build a hive mind for ships
startups-vc.summary: An Arlington, Virginia-based maritime technology startup secured $42 million in funding to deploy sensor networks on ships that enhance vessel tracking capabilities beyond current AIS technology. The funding underscores venture capital's interest in modernizing maritime operations.

startups-vc.title: Clouted wants to take the guesswork out of making short videos go viral
startups-vc.summary: Video clipping startup Clouted raised $7 million in seed funding led by Slow Ventures to develop technology that optimizes short-form video content for viral potential. The funding reflects continued investor focus on creator economy and content distribution solutions.

startups-vc.title: After A 6-Figure Fertility Journey, This Founder Built An IVF Startup With 'Outcome Protection'
startups-vc.summary: Fertility startup Gaia deployed artificial intelligence and machine learning trained on millions of anonymized fertility outcomes to assess risk and probability for IVF treatment. The venture represents emerging investor interest in AI-driven healthcare solutions addressing reproductive medicine.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Pioneering trial for treating genetic disease before birth nears reality
health-biotech.summary: A UCSF research team has submitted an FDA application seeking approval for the first clinical trial of in utero gene therapy targeting a rare lysosomal storage disorder. This landmark development represents a significant advance in prenatal genetic medicine and could establish a new treatment paradigm for inherited genetic diseases.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Scientists track cellular disruptions that lead to type 1 diabetes
health-biotech.summary: Emerging research indicates that sustained viral infections in genetically predisposed individuals trigger the cellular mechanisms underlying type 1 diabetes development. These findings could inform prevention strategies and early intervention approaches for the autoimmune disorder.

health-biotech.title: Pfizer's next-gen pneumonia shot beats Prevnar 20; Relay targets $175M
health-biotech.summary: Pfizer's experimental 25-valent pneumococcal vaccine candidate demonstrated superior immunogenicity compared to the approved 20-valent Prevnar 20 in a Phase 2 head-to-head trial. The advancement represents meaningful progress toward broader pneumococcal disease protection.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Biotech execs, academic expert lament impact of FDA turnover on rare disease drug development
health-biotech.summary: Rare disease biotech leaders and regulatory experts have expressed concern that recent departures of key FDA officials have created significant uncertainty in the drug approval process. The regulatory instability threatens to disrupt development timelines for treatments serving vulnerable patient populations with limited therapeutic options.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Kennedy fires two leaders on preventive services task force
health-biotech.summary: Kennedy has removed two vice chairs from the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force and positioned himself to substantially reshape the panel that determines government-covered preventive care recommendations. These personnel changes signal potential shifts in preventive health policy and insurance coverage standards.

health-biotech.title: Congressional Democrats try to force a vote to end Medicare AI prior authorization pilot
health-biotech.summary: Democratic lawmakers are pursuing efforts to terminate a Medicare experiment employing artificial intelligence to determine care approvals and denials. The legislative push reflects ongoing concerns about automated decision-making in healthcare coverage determinations.

health-biotech.title: Surgeon general's office issues warning on screen time for children
health-biotech.summary: The U.S. Surgeon General has issued a public health advisory calling on parents, educators, and policymakers to reduce children's screen exposure, citing evidence that certain usage patterns pose demonstrable health risks to pediatric populations. The warning underscores growing concerns about digital media's impact on child development and mental health.

health-biotech.title: Fetal gene therapy's progress calls into question the taboo on embryo editing
health-biotech.summary: Scientific advances in prenatal gene therapy have prompted some researchers to reconsider established ethical and regulatory constraints on human embryo editing. The evolving sentiment reflects tensions between therapeutic potential and ongoing bioethical concerns in reproductive genetics.

health-biotech.title: Driven by GLP-1s, pharma's relationship with consumers is starting to change
health-biotech.summary: Pharmaceutical companies are expanding direct-to-consumer engagement channels in response to growing consumer demand for GLP-1 weight-loss medications and other therapies. The trend reflects shifting patient expectations and the emergence of alternative distribution pathways beyond traditional physician-mediated channels.

health-biotech.title: I&I biotech cAMPfield attracts top-tier investors in $180M Series A
health-biotech.summary: San Diego-based cAMPfield Therapeutics has secured $180 million in Series A funding from prominent investors to advance its inflammatory and immunology drug candidates licensed from established pharmaceutical partners. The financing demonstrates continued investor appetite for asset-licensing biotech models in immunology.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group executed a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes across U.S. school districts and universities. The attackers defaced the login page with ransom demands and threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service operation attributed to threat actor Fox Tempest that weaponized Microsoft's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware attacks. The operation compromised thousands of machines and networks globally.

cybersecurity.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
cybersecurity.summary: GitHub disclosed unauthorized access to its internal repositories following a threat actor breach, with the notorious group TeamPCP listing the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on cybercrime forums. The company stated it has found no evidence of impact to customer information stored outside GitHub's internal repositories.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: German authorities identified and exposed Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin, a 31-year-old Russian national who operated under the alias "UNKN" and headed the ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil. Shchukin was responsible for coordinating at least 130 computer sabotage and extortion attacks against victims between 2019 and 2021.

cybersecurity.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed a large-scale ad fraud and malvertising operation called Trapdoor targeting Android users through 455 malicious apps and 183 command-and-control domains. The operation functioned as a multi-stage fraud pipeline, generating 659 million fraudulent daily bid requests.

cybersecurity.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers identified renewed activity from China-aligned threat actor Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that leverage Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control communications. The group, active since at least 2022, continues targeting government agencies with sophisticated attack infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
cybersecurity.summary: Grafana Labs reported that an investigation into its breach revealed no compromise of customer production systems, with the incident limited to its GitHub environment containing public and private source code. The scope included internal GitHub repositories affected through the TanStack npm supply chain attack vector.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Releases Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Bypass CVE-2026-45585 Exploit
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released a mitigation for the YellowKey BitLocker bypass vulnerability following public disclosure of the zero-day flaw. Tracked as CVE-2026-45585 with a CVSS score of 6.8, the vulnerability represents a security feature bypass in Windows encryption.

cybersecurity.title: Typosquatting Is No Longer a User Problem. It's a Supply Chain Problem
cybersecurity.summary: Attackers are embedding AI-generated lookalike domains within third-party scripts running on web properties, transforming typosquatting from a user-level threat into a supply chain vulnerability. Current detection stacks lack visibility into this evolving attack vector embedded within legitimate third-party code.

cybersecurity.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
cybersecurity.summary: Proof-of-concept exploit code has been released for a patched Linux kernel security flaw that enables local privilege escalation attacks. The vulnerability, dubbed DirtyDecrypt or DirtyCBC, was reported by Zellic and V12 security researchers and poses risks to systems running vulnerable kernel versions.

energy-climate.title: New coal plants hit '10-year' global high in 2025 – but power output still fell
energy-climate.summary: Global coal-fired power plant construction reached a decade-high in 2025, yet overall coal power output declined, reflecting ongoing tension between fossil fuel investment and renewable energy displacement. This paradox underscores the complexity of the global energy transition despite increased renewable capacity additions.

energy-climate.title: U.S. PV manufacturing capex could reach $7 billion in 2027 in breakout year for domestic supply-chain
energy-climate.summary: U.S. solar manufacturing capital expenditure is projected to surge 150% year-on-year to $7 billion in 2027, driven by major investments from Tesla and Corning. This represents a significant acceleration in domestic solar supply-chain development and the scaling of silicon-based photovoltaic technology.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 8 May 2026: EU eyes fossil-fuel exemptions | Wind and solar save UK '£1.7bn' | Amazon 'tipping point'
energy-climate.summary: Key weekly climate developments include the EU considering fossil-fuel exemptions in its climate policy framework, wind and solar energy delivering £1.7 billion in savings to the UK, and renewed concerns about Amazon rainforest tipping points. The digest highlights critical policy shifts and renewable energy economic benefits amid climate mitigation efforts.

energy-climate.title: Italy to assign 10 GW of PV in 2026-2027 renewables auctions
energy-climate.summary: Italy's energy agency GSE announced plans to allocate 10 gigawatts of solar capacity and 16 gigawatts of wind power through competitive auctions over 2026-2027. This substantial renewable energy procurement reflects Italy's commitment to accelerating its clean energy transition.

energy-climate.title: PV curtailment on the rise in India
energy-climate.summary: India curtailed 300 gigawatt-hours of renewable energy in the first quarter of 2026 due to transmission infrastructure constraints, according to analysis from Ember. The curtailment highlights a critical infrastructure bottleneck limiting India's ability to fully utilize its expanding renewable capacity.

energy-climate.title: China conducts first experiments for space-based solar power plants
energy-climate.summary: China's Sun Chasing project has successfully conducted early tests of space-based solar power systems, demonstrating over 100-meter wireless power transmission with efficiencies delivering up to 1,180 watts. The technology represents a long-term exploration of novel renewable energy approaches with potential applications for continuous orbital and ground-based power supply.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: What the UK car industry is not saying about EV targets
energy-climate.summary: This analysis examines claims from the UK automotive industry regarding electric vehicle adoption targets, revealing gaps between industry rhetoric and market realities. The investigation challenges widely-cited arguments against EV transition timelines in the UK.

energy-climate.title: From coal to solar: A stable jobs transition for Poland
energy-climate.summary: Polish research demonstrates that the nation's solar industry expansion could generate between 20,000 and 40,000 direct full-time jobs through 2040, contingent on investment and operations levels. The analysis suggests a viable economic pathway for workforce transition from declining coal sectors to growing renewable energy operations.

energy-climate.title: Common‑sense state action can unlock a geothermal revolution in Utah and beyond
energy-climate.summary: Strategic policy coordination pairing geothermal development with transmission infrastructure acceleration can unlock gigawatt-scale geothermal potential across the U.S. West, according to analysis from the Clean Air Task Force. Regional coordination and streamlined permitting are identified as key enablers for geothermal expansion.

energy-climate.title: Researchers develop perovskite solar cells about 50 times thinner than conventional devices
energy-climate.summary: Researchers at Nanyang Technological University Singapore have engineered ultra-thin perovskite solar cells using vacuum-based thermal evaporation, achieving devices 50 times thinner than conventional silicon cells with semi-transparent and diffuse-light-responsive capabilities. The breakthrough shows promise for building-integrated photovoltaic applications in windows and building facades.

asia-pacific.title: The weight of silk: what the honour means for the Inner Bar as an institution
asia-pacific.summary:
Few moments at the Bar are more joyful and surreal than watching one’s former pupil take silk: pride, disbelief and the sudden realisation that someone you still instinctively think of as “junior” is now being bestowed with unmistakable senior seriousness.
Last Saturday was, therefore, a sentimental moment for me as I watched my former pupil, Bonnie Cheng, being honoured with the new status at the ceremony.
As the old saying goes, every barrister wants to take silk – until one does. The great...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong crews fight flooding with robotic ‘dragons’ after intense downpours
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong emergency crews worked around the clock with robotic “water-pumping dragons” to clear widespread flooding in the northern New Territories after intense downpours triggered a brief red rainstorm warning early Thursday.
The Hong Kong Observatory issued the red rainstorm signal at 2.40am as intense showers battered the city, following an amber warning that had been in force since 9pm on Wednesday and a flooding alert for the northern New Territories.
It said heavy showers and squally...

asia-pacific.title: US to send Typhon, Himars missile systems to Japan for drills amid China tensions
asia-pacific.summary:
The US military will deploy its Typhon midrange missile launcher and Himars rocket system to southwestern Japan next month for joint exercises with the Self-Defence Forces, sources familiar with the matter said on Wednesday, in a move apparently aimed at deterring China.
The systems will be deployed to the Maritime Self-Defence Force’s Kanoya Airbase in Kagoshima prefecture during the Valiant Shield and Orient Shield joint exercises between June and September, the sources said.
The Typhon...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong helpers cry out as popular garden in Central to close for underpass work
asia-pacific.summary:
A Hong Kong migrant workers’ group has urged authorities to expand a pedestrian area in Central on Sundays, as a section of a landmark garden that serves as a popular gathering spot for domestic helpers will temporarily close for the construction of a pedestrian underpass.
The subway is part of the tender requirements for a Central harbourfront site secured by developer Henderson Land Development.
It will run below Statue Square Gardens and connect the Central MTR station at Chater Road to the..

asia-pacific.title: China beauty influencer returns after 3-year hiatus, gaining 10 million followers in a day
asia-pacific.summary:
A former top beauty make-up influencer who has made a whirlwind return to social media after disappearing from view has called on her fans to place their trust in her.
The influencer, who previously used the handle @chengshian and had a combined 30 million followers on several leading platforms in China, stopped releasing videos in May 2023, news website Sina reported.
She returned to the spotlight on May 13 by opening a new account @Jiang Chenglan, which is her real name. She then amassed 10...

asia-pacific.title: As war engulfs the Middle East, China’s Xinjiang is thriving with future tech
asia-pacific.summary:
As the war in Iran disrupts global oil and chemical supplies, China’s coal-heavy energy sector is seizing an unprecedented opportunity. Dannie Peng visits the Changji Hui autonomous prefecture in Xinjiang – one of the country’s four major bases for large-scale, modern coal-chemical production – to see first-hand how a vast industrial ecosystem is rapidly taking shape on the ground.
A four-hour drive northeast of Urumqi, the regional capital of Xinjiang, leads to a colossal open-pit mine. There,.

asia-pacific.title: Singapore’s weaponised drone trial spotlights regional rush for unmanned systems
asia-pacific.summary:
Singapore’s plan to trial weaponised unmanned drones has put a spotlight on Southeast Asia’s fast-growing interest in systems that can track and strike at targets without putting operators directly in harm’s way.
But as countries race to upgrade their drone and counter-drone capabilities, analysts say regulation, transparency and confidence-building will be crucial to prevent accidents or miscalculations that could fuel regional tensions.
Singapore announced on Thursday that it would start...

asia-pacific.title: How Trump’s revenge tour against Republicans could backfire in the midterms
asia-pacific.summary:
Sixteen months into his second presidency, Donald Trump may ⁠be as unpopular as he has ever been among the American electorate – but his grip on his ⁠core base of Maga voters remains unshaken.
That power was on full display over the last two weeks, as Trump ousted a string of fellow Republicans he considered apostates for not showing him enough personal fealty.
The president’s revenge tour continued on Tuesday, when a hand-picked loyalist defeated US congressman Thomas Massie, a frequent critic,

asia-pacific.title: With China poised for record trade surplus, former Chongqing mayor calls for action
asia-pacific.summary:
China needs a coordinated policy package of currency appreciation, tariff cuts and elevated labour benefits to rebalance its massive trade surplus, outspoken former Chongqing mayor Huang Qifan said on Tuesday.
Speaking at this year’s Tsinghua PBCSF Global Finance Forum in Chengdu, the capital of the southwestern province of Sichuan, Huang described China’s 2025 record-breaking US$1.2 trillion merchandise trade surplus as “shocking”.
That momentum has shown no signs of slowing. China’s goods...

asia-pacific.title: Chinese national charged in alleged scheme to smuggle drug shipments to US
asia-pacific.summary:
In a sign of growing cooperation between the US and China in the wake of last week’s summit in Beijing, the US Justice Department announced on Wednesday the arrest in China of an alleged drug smuggler wanted by the US in connection with a sizeable shipment of illegal drugs into the US state of Georgia.
As outlined in court records, Wei Gong, also known as David Gong, allegedly imported more than 10 kilograms of various potent stimulant drugs, which he allegedly sold to an undercover agent and...
